name: Chocolate PB/Jeebies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsweetened Dutch process cocoa powder
1 1/2 cups Firmly Packed Light Brown Sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
2. Sift together cocoa powder, flour, baking soda, and salt onto a sheet of wax paper. Set aside.
3. To prepare the chocolate mixture: Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom of a double boiler over medium heat. Place semisweet and unsweetened baking chocolate in the top half of the double boiler. Stir until smooth. Keep at room temperature or melt in a microwave.
4. In the bowl of an electric mixer, beat butter and brown sugar on medium speed for 1 minute. Scrape down the sides of the bowl, then beat on high speed for an additional 30 seconds. Add eggs one at a time, beating on medium after each addition, until combined. Stir in vanilla extract and beat on medium for another 30 seconds.
5. Add melted chocolate mixture to the batter and beat on low until smooth. Scrape down the sides of the bowl and beat for another 30 seconds.
6. Gradually add the flour mixture to the batter, mixing until thoroughly combined (about 20-30 seconds).
7. Stir in chocolate chips and peanut butter chips. Drop heaping tablespoons onto ungreased baking sheets, forming about 6 cookies per sheet (each cookie is roughly 2 teaspoons). Place the sheets in the middle of the oven.
8. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes or until lightly golden. Cool cookies on the pans for 5 to 6 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
[To prepare Chocolate Dipping Sauce: In a saucepan, heat cream, butter, and sugar over medium-high heat. Stir until the sugar dissolves. Bring to a boil, then remove from heat. Place chocolate in a stainless steel bowl and pour boiling cream over it. Let stand for 5 minutes before stirring until smooth. Cool to room temperature. The dip can be made up to 3-4 days ahead and refrigerated. Reheat gently, stirring, and bring back to room temperature before serving.]
Makes about 2 1/2 cups of dipping sauce. Makes approximately 3 dozen cookies.
